2026-03-06.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz Feb 28, VLCC rates record high
2026-03-06.exit_case: Iran lifts the blockade or a diplomatic deal formally reopens the Strait — no evidence of either yet; closure fully active.
2026-03-06.assessment: Week 1 / entry week. Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz (in force since Feb 28 following US-Israeli strikes) is fully live with zero sign of diplomatic resolution. The VLCC benchmark rate Gulf-to-China hit an all-time high of $423,736/day — directly, mechanically tied to this chokepoint closure. CNBC's coverage explicitly labels BWET 'under-owned,' a strong signal the position is early-stage before mainstream crowding. All five vehicles are clean pure-plays on the same acute catalyst: rising VLCC spot rates driven by this specific blockade. The catalyst is a classic ACUTE binary — blockade persists (rates elevated, thesis lives) or it ends (hard exit, same day). Retaining full basket; no vehicle thesis has broken. Monitor: any formal Iran-US diplomatic talks, partial Strait reopening for non-US-flagged vessels, or sharp VLCC rate reversal as leading indicators of resolution.
2026-03-06.exit_advice: Hard exit the moment the Strait reopens (ceasefire, US-Iran deal, Iranian back-down) or VLCC spot rates collapse sharply from current highs signaling imminent resolution. Do NOT exit on silence or mere diplomatic rhetoric without a signed/announced reopening.

2026-03-20.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz Feb 28, VLCC rates record high
2026-03-20.exit_case: VLCC rates may have quietly normalized if blockade softened — no dedicated tanker press in three weeks.
2026-03-20.assessment: The MarketScreener piece (Mar 16) explicitly names investor attention on 'developments in the Middle East, particularly the Strait of Hormuz' as the live driver lifting FTSE 100 energy stocks. This is fresh on-thesis Strait coverage — per the SILENCE DECAY rule, a single fresh trend-story resets conviction back up from last week's 7 to 8. No reopening, no diplomatic framework, no VLCC rate reversal appears anywhere in this week's news feed. The blockade that began Feb 28 remains the live acute catalyst. Notably, the Strait reference is still embedded in secondary equity coverage (not a dedicated tanker / shipping headline), indicating the position has not yet hit mainstream saturation — the tanker bull-run narrative has not been plastered across front pages. All five names remain clean pure-plays on the same chokepoint driver; no individual vehicle thesis has broken. The exit_case above is the honest best argument against — three weeks without a dedicated VLCC rate print in press leaves open the possibility of quiet normalization — but the MarketScreener confirmation of active Strait concern directly rebuts it. Monitor: any Iran-US back-channel talks, partial Strait passage for neutral-flag hulls, Baltic Exchange VLCC rate reversal, or a dedicated tanker/shipping front-page story signaling mainstream crowding.
2026-03-20.exit_advice: Hold full basket. Exit only on confirmed Strait reopening, formal Iran-US ceasefire, or sharp Baltic Exchange VLCC rate reversal reported in press.

The 'Trump vows escalation before exit' Australian headlines are tariff-context stories, not Hormuz-related, and do not qualify as on-thesis coverage. The thesis is alive but conviction is fading purely on silence; the position is approaching cull-competitive territory if fresh milestone coverage does not resume. All five vehicles — BWET, DHT, FRO, NAT, STNG — remain clean pure-plays on the same chokepoint/VLCC-rate driver; no individual name's own thesis has broken. Watch: any Iran-US back-channel talks, partial Strait reopening for neutral-flag hulls, Baltic Exchange VLCC rate prints reversing, or a dedicated tanker/shipping front-page story signaling mainstream saturation.
2026-04-03.exit_advice: Hold. A Strait of Hormuz reopening is a front-page global event that cannot pass silently — its absence in any feed is continued confirmation the blockade persists. Do NOT exit on silence alone. Hard exit triggers: (1) any Iran-US diplomatic agreement or formal ceasefire announced, (2) Strait declared open for commercial transit, (3) Baltic Exchange VLCC rates reverse sharply and a dedicated shipping headline confirms normalization.

2026-04-10.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz Feb 28, VLCC rates record high
2026-04-10.exit_case: US-Iran two-week ceasefire agreed April 8; Strait of Hormuz expected to reopen, crude fell — blockade reversed.
2026-04-10.assessment: Week 6 — HARD EXIT. Two sources lock in the resolution. Yahoo Finance (Apr 8) reported futures sliding as 'Trump's Hormuz deadline loomed,' confirming the blockade was fully live through April 7. Then RTE (Apr 8) reported the ceasefire agreement, with crude prices already falling on Strait-reopening expectations. Baystreet (Apr 7) additionally confirmed Iran was still refusing to relent as recently as Tuesday — so the reversal is fresh and clean, not a stale priced-in event. Per the rules, a ceasefire that reopens a chokepoint is the textbook hard exit: the driving condition (Strait blockade in force since Feb 28) has reversed, the market has repriced crude and equities same-day, and VLCC rates will follow. The silence-decay path (conviction tracking 8→7→8→7→6 entering this week) is now irrelevant — catalyst_resolved=TRUE overrides everything. The entire basket was entered on the same acute chokepoint catalyst; all five names must be exited together.
2026-04-10.exit_advice: EXIT ALL POSITIONS IMMEDIATELY. The driving condition has reversed: RTE (Apr 8) confirms 'the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, sending crude prices lower on expectations that energy supplies through the Strait of Hormuz would resume.' This is the exact hard-exit trigger identified at entry — ceasefire is explicitly listed in the rules as a driving-condition reversal. VLCC rates, which hit ATH $423,736/day on the blockade, will collapse as routing normalises. Do not wait for a two-week clock to expire; the market has already priced the reopening (crude down, equities up). Exit BWET, DHT, FRO, NAT, STNG.

2026-05-08.thesis: Oklo May 12 scheduled catalyst event after 46% April surge
2026-05-08.exit_case: OKLO already surged 46% in April, potentially front-running and fully pricing in the May 12 event.
2026-05-08.assessment: First week on position. The May 12 scheduled catalyst is explicitly unresolved — Motley Fool (2026-05-08) directly flags it as the next potential inflection point ('Is the nuclear energy stock set for another big jump after May 12?'), confirming the market views it as a live, pending event. Devil's advocate case is real but not decisive: the 46% April pre-run elevates the expectations bar and compresses upside asymmetry, creating genuine 'buy the rumor, sell the news' risk. However, the catalyst has not occurred, there is no reversal, and the concrete scheduled date keeps thesis_live. Retail coverage (multiple Motley Fool pieces this week on nuclear energy broadly) is building but not yet mainstream-saturated — no front-page frenzy, no sell-side target-raising parade. Conviction set at 6: specific, datable, still-pending catalyst with a clean pure-play vehicle, discounted slightly for the pre-event run-up compressing remaining edge. Hold through May 12; reassess immediately post-event.
2026-05-08.exit_advice: Exit on or immediately after May 12 once the scheduled event (likely earnings or key business announcement) resolves — the catalyst is a single datable event; hold through market noise until then, but do not linger post-resolution.

2026-05-15.thesis: Oklo May 12 scheduled catalyst event after 46% April surge
2026-05-15.exit_case: May 12 scheduled catalyst date has passed — the specific datable acute event has occurred and resolved.
2026-05-15.assessment: Journal entry from 2026-05-08 was explicit: 'Hold through May 12; reassess immediately post-event.' We are now post-May 12. The acute catalyst — a specific scheduled date the Motley Fool flagged as 'the next potential inflection point' — has occurred. Catalyst_resolved must be TRUE. The only OKLO-specific news this week is JPMorgan declining to turn bullish after the event, confirming the market absorbed the May 12 event without a new inflection. No follow-on concrete scheduled catalyst is cited in any source. The 46% April pre-run elevated the expectations bar; with the event now behind us and sell-side underwhelmed, the original asymmetry argument is spent. Exit cleanly rather than migrate into a different (structural SMR) thesis under the same ticker.
2026-05-15.exit_advice: Exit OKLO. The entire thesis was a single, datable, acute event — the May 12 scheduled catalyst. That date has now passed. Per the rules for acute catalysts, once the specific event occurs the thesis is resolved and the edge is gone. The post-event JPMorgan piece ('stops short of turning bullish on OKLO despite strong SMR outlook') is the clearest possible signal: the sell-side reviewed the event and is not compelled to upgrade — no bullish inflection materialized. No new concrete scheduled catalyst appears in any this-week coverage. The broader nuclear/SMR structural theme remains alive, but that is a separate, different thesis — not the one entered on. Close the position.
